---
title: "Deep Learning Roadmap"
---

## Overview

This document tracks remaining development tasks for the Deep Learning library and widget. 
The core library (encoders, decoders, model abstraction, multi-backend inference, registry, 
runtime JSON models, and widget UI) is complete. The items below represent outstanding work 
for full production readiness.

## Remaining Tasks

### Integration Testing

End-to-end tests that exercise the full pipeline with real model weights are still needed. 
These require Python model export as a prerequisite.

- [ ] **Export test models** ‚Äî Create a simple model, export as both `.pt` (TorchScript) and 
  `.pt2` (AOT Inductor), and commit the exported files as test fixtures 
  (see [AOT Inductor Tutorial](aot_inductor_tutorial.qmd))
- [ ] **Integration test: TorchScript backend** ‚Äî Load a `.pt` file, run inference, verify 
  output tensor shape and value range
- [ ] **Integration test: AOT Inductor backend** ‚Äî Load a `.pt2` file, run inference, verify 
  output tensor shape and value range
- [ ] **Integration test: NeuroSAM full pipeline** ‚Äî Load NeuroSAM weights, bind inputs, run 
  inference, verify output written to DataManager
- [ ] **End-to-end UI test** ‚Äî Select a model in the widget, configure bindings, execute 
  inference, verify results appear in DataManager

### Input Pipeline Architecture

The input system supports three distinct input modes: **Dynamic**, **Static**, and 
**Recurrent**. Each mode has different batch-size implications, different assembly 
semantics in `SlotAssembler`, and different UI requirements. This section tracks the 
staged implementation plan.

#### Phase 1: Dynamic Input Time Offsets

Dynamic inputs (`SlotBindingData`) currently read at `current_frame + batch_index`. 
Adding a `time_offset` field lets users specify temporal offsets (e.g., "read one frame 
behind") without the static input machinery.

- [ ] **Add `time_offset` to `SlotBindingData`** ‚Äî New `int time_offset = 0` field. During 
  assembly, each frame encodes at `current_frame + batch_index + time_offset`
- [ ] **Update `SlotAssembler::assembleInputs()`** ‚Äî Apply `time_offset` in the dynamic 
  input encoding loop
- [ ] **UI: time offset spinbox** ‚Äî Add a spinbox for each dynamic input slot in the 
  Properties widget
- [ ] **Tests** ‚Äî Verify that negative offsets clamp to frame 0, positive offsets 
  clamp to max frame

#### Phase 2: Static Input Capture & Preview

`StaticInputData` currently re-encodes at `current_frame + time_offset` every invocation. 
True "capture once, reuse forever" requires caching the encoded tensor.

- [ ] **Absolute vs relative capture mode** ‚Äî Add a `capture_mode` field to 
  `StaticInputData`: `Relative` (current behavior, re-encodes each run at 
  `current_frame + time_offset`) vs `Absolute` (encode once at a fixed frame, cache 
  the tensor)
- [ ] **Tensor cache in SlotAssembler** ‚Äî Store captured tensors in 
  `map<string, Tensor> _static_cache` keyed by `slot_name + memory_index`. Skip 
  re-encoding when a cached tensor exists for an `Absolute`-mode entry
- [ ] **"Capture Current Frame" button** ‚Äî Widget action: encodes the current frame's data 
  for a static slot and stores it in the cache. User navigates to the desired frame, 
  clicks capture, and the tensor is frozen
- [ ] **Static tensor preview in ViewWidget** ‚Äî Render color-mapped thumbnails of cached 
  static tensors so the user can verify what the model will see as its memory input
- [ ] **Batch dimension expansion** ‚Äî For `Absolute`-mode static inputs, `.expand()` the 
  cached tensor along the batch dimension so batch_size can be > 1
- [ ] **Cache invalidation** ‚Äî Clear cached tensors when the user changes `data_key`, 
  re-captures, or switches models

#### Phase 3: Multi-Frame Sequence Inputs

Support stacking multiple captured frames along a slot's `sequence_dim` axis. This 
enables architectures like NeuroSAM that consume N memory frames (e.g., 4 reference 
images along a sequence dimension).

- [ ] **Sequence packing** ‚Äî When `TensorSlotDescriptor::sequence_dim >= 0`, assemble 
  multiple `StaticInputData` entries along that axis using `memory_index` to place 
  each capture at the correct position
- [ ] **UI: sequence entry manager** ‚Äî Add/remove/reorder buttons for sequence slots in 
  the Properties widget. Each entry shows its `memory_index`, source `data_key`, and 
  captured frame number
- [ ] **Variable-length sequences** ‚Äî Handle cases where the user captures fewer frames 
  than the sequence dimension size (zero-pad unused positions) and surface a warning
- [ ] **Boolean mask alignment** ‚Äî Ensure `is_boolean_mask` slots with `sequence_dim` 
  correctly set active/inactive flags for each sequence position

#### Phase 4: Recurrent (Feedback) Inputs

Map a model output slot back to an input slot for sequential frame-by-frame processing. 
The prediction at frame t becomes an input at frame t+1.

- [ ] **`RecurrentBindingData` struct** ‚Äî New binding type with fields: `input_slot_name`, 
  `output_slot_name`, `init_mode` (`"zeros"` | `"static_capture"` | `"first_output"`), 
  optional `init_data_key` and `init_frame` for the `static_capture` init mode
- [ ] **Sequential inference loop** ‚Äî When recurrent bindings exist, `SlotAssembler` runs 
  a sequential loop: for each frame, assemble inputs (injecting the previous output 
  tensor into the recurrent input slot), call `forward()`, decode, carry the output 
  tensor forward
- [ ] **t=0 initialization** ‚Äî Implement the three init strategies:
    - `zeros`: start with an all-zeros tensor matching the input slot shape
    - `static_capture`: use a user-provided captured tensor (e.g., ground-truth mask at 
      a reference frame)
    - `first_output`: run the model once with zeros, discard the decoded result, use the 
      raw output tensor as the initial state for the real sequence
- [ ] **Auto-constrain batch_size = 1** ‚Äî When any `RecurrentBindingData` is present, 
  force `batch_size = 1` in the widget and disable the batch size spinbox. Display 
  an explanatory tooltip
- [ ] **UI: recurrent binding panel** ‚Äî Combo boxes to map an output slot to an input 
  slot, plus init mode selector and optional capture controls for `static_capture`
- [ ] **Progress reporting** ‚Äî Show per-frame progress bar during sequential recurrent 
  inference (frame N of M)

#### Phase 5: Hybrid Sequence Inputs (Static + Recurrent)

A single sequence slot (e.g., `memory_images` with shape `[5, 3, 256, 256]` and 
`sequence_dim=0`) may need mixed sourcing: some positions filled from static captures 
and others fed recurrently from the model's previous output. Example: 4 user-selected 
reference frames at positions 0‚Äì3 and the model's own prediction at position 4.

- [ ] **Per-position source tagging** ‚Äî Extend the binding model so each position along 
  `sequence_dim` independently declares its source. `StaticInputData` entries already 
  target a `memory_index`; add an optional `target_memory_index` to 
  `RecurrentBindingData` so it can inject into a specific sequence position rather than 
  replacing the entire slot tensor
- [ ] **Hybrid tensor assembly** ‚Äî Update `SlotAssembler` to compose a single sequence 
  tensor from mixed sources in one pass:
    1. Start with the full `[batch, seq, ...]` tensor (zeros)
    2. Fill static positions from the tensor cache (Phase 2/3)
    3. Fill recurrent position(s) from the carried-forward output tensor (Phase 4)
    4. Validate: error if any position is claimed by both a static entry and a recurrent 
       binding, or if a position has no source at all (warn on zero-filled gaps)
- [ ] **t=0 init for recurrent positions only** ‚Äî In hybrid mode, the init strategy 
  (`zeros`, `static_capture`, `first_output`) applies only to the recurrent position(s). 
  Static positions are always populated regardless of frame index
- [ ] **Batch constraint propagation** ‚Äî Hybrid slots with any recurrent position force 
  batch=1 (same as Phase 4). If all recurrent positions are removed, batch constraint 
  relaxes back to the static/dynamic default
- [ ] **UI: unified sequence editor** ‚Äî Each row in the sequence entry manager (Phase 3) 
  gains a source-type selector: **Static** (capture controls) or **Recurrent** (output 
  slot + init mode). The panel visually distinguishes the two types (e.g., static rows 
  show the captured frame thumbnail, recurrent rows show the output mapping)
- [ ] **Validation & diagnostics** ‚Äî At assembly time, warn if the recurrent output 
  tensor shape doesn't match the target sequence position slice. Surface mismatches in 
  the Properties widget status area

#### Phase 6: Batch Size & AOT Inductor Compatibility

Different input modes impose different batch-size constraints. This phase ensures the 
model compilation story and multi-mode switching are clean.

- [ ] **Model batch mode metadata** ‚Äî Extend `ModelBase` (or `TensorSlotDescriptor`) with 
  a `BatchMode` concept: `Fixed(N)`, `Dynamic(min, max)`, or `RecurrentOnly` (fixed 1). 
  Models report which modes they support
- [ ] **Multi-variant model loading** ‚Äî Support loading separate `.pt2` files for 
  different batch configurations (e.g., `neurosam_recurrent.pt2` compiled with batch=1, 
  `neurosam_batched.pt2` compiled with batch=8 or dynamic batch). The widget 
  auto-selects the variant based on active bindings
- [ ] **Dynamic-shape AOT export documentation** ‚Äî Document the `torch.export` workflow 
  with `dynamic_shapes={...: {0: Dim("batch")}}` for users who want a single `.pt2` 
  supporting variable batch sizes
- [ ] **Widget batch-size logic** ‚Äî Auto-detect constraints: recurrent bindings ‚Üí batch=1; 
  all-static or all-dynamic ‚Üí clamp to `model.maxBatchSize()`; mixed ‚Üí use the most 
  restrictive constraint. Surface the reasoning in the UI

### Batch Inference

The widget currently supports single-frame inference. Batch processing over multiple frames 
is partially implemented.

- [ ] **Implement "Run Batch" action** ‚Äî Loop over `batch_size` consecutive frames, encode 
  each at its batch index, call `forward()` once, decode each output element back into 
  the DataManager. The `_onRunBatch()` handler currently shows a "not yet implemented" dialog.
- [ ] **Progress reporting** ‚Äî Show a progress bar in the View widget during batch processing
- [ ] **TimeFrame integration** ‚Äî Ensure batch inference correctly resolves time offsets for 
  memory/static inputs relative to each frame being processed

### Tensor Preview Visualization

The View widget has placeholder areas for tensor channel previews.

- [ ] **Tensor channel thumbnails** ‚Äî Render color-mapped previews of each input tensor 
  channel after encoding (pre-inference visualization)
- [ ] **Output overlay** ‚Äî Overlay decoded output (mask, points, lines) on the source image 
  in the View widget

### Widget Polish

- [ ] **State persistence** ‚Äî Verify full save/restore of `DeepLearningState` across 
  application sessions (slot bindings, weights path, batch size, static inputs)
- [ ] **Error handling UX** ‚Äî Surface model loading errors and inference failures as 
  user-friendly status messages in the Properties widget
- [ ] **Model hot-reload** ‚Äî Detect when the weights file changes on disk and offer to 
  reload

### Model Export Documentation

- [ ] **User-facing export guide** ‚Äî Document the Python export workflow in the user guide 
  for users who train their own models
- [ ] **Multi-model `.pt2` packages** ‚Äî Document and test loading multiple models from a 
  single `.pt2` archive

### Additional Encoder/Decoder Support

- [ ] **Distance mode** for `Line2DEncoder` ‚Äî Distance transform instead of binary/heatmap
- [ ] **Multi-peak `TensorToPoint2D`** ‚Äî Expose multi-peak detection parameters in the 
  widget UI (currently only available programmatically)
- [ ] **`TensorToAnalogTimeSeries`** decoder ‚Äî For models that output scalar time series 
  rather than spatial maps

### Performance

- [ ] **Async inference** ‚Äî Add `forwardAsync()` returning `std::future` to `ModelBase` for 
  non-blocking inference during batch processing
- [ ] **GPU memory management** ‚Äî Track and display GPU memory usage in the widget; warn 
  when approaching limits
- [ ] **Benchmark suite** ‚Äî Benchmark encode/decode throughput and end-to-end inference 
  latency for representative models
